bio.education: Newport Harbor High School Long Beach State, Concordia University
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Bob “Butch” May and Barbara May
  • Father, Butch, was a member of the 1968 U.S. Men’s National Volleyball Team and mother, Barbara, was a former nationally ranked tennis player
  • Has two brothers, Brack and Scott
  • Married to former MLB player Matt Treanor
  • Has three children, Malia Barbara and fraternal twins, Mele Elizarin and Mia Kanoelani
  • Published autobiography, “Misty, Digging Deep in Volleyball and Life” in 2010
  • Has tattoos honoring her deceased mother and grandmother
  • After retiring from competition following the Olympic Games London 2012, coached at the junior and collegiate levels
  • At the time of her induction into the USOPC Hall of Fame, served as the director of volleyball operations for Long Beach City College and as a co-chair to the President’s Council on Sports, Fitness and Nutrition.
Olympic Experience
  • 4-time Olympian; 3-time Olympic medalist (3 gold)
    • Olympic Games London 2012, gold (Team - Women)
    • Olympic Games Beijing 2008, gold (Team - Women)
    • Olympic Games Athens 2004, gold (Team - Women)
    • Olympic Games Sydney 2000, 5th (Team - Women)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2011 – silver (Team - Women)
  • Years of participation: Team - Women 2001, 2007, 2011
  • Medals: 2 (1 gold, 1 silver)
    • Gold – 2007 (Team - Women)
    • Silver – 2011 (Team - Women)

Quick Facts
  • Retired after the 2018 beach season to coach beach volleyball at Loyola Marymount University
  • Married to dermatologist Dr. Paola Rodriguez. The couple has a daughter
Career Highlights
  • Beach Volleyball Head Coach at Loyola Marymount University in California since 2015
  • Top international finishes are two silvers on the NORCECA Continental Tour
  • Made his professional debut in 2003 at a domestic AVP event
  • ---
  • DID YOU KNOW?
  • Played in five 2018 FIVB World Tour events with Trevor Crabb, winning Lucerne 3-star and JinJiang 2-star
  • In 2017, tied for 17th in his lone FIVB World Tour event, earned silver on the NORCECA Continental Tour; On the domestic AVP Tour, placed third three times
  • Played on the FIVB World Tour with Ryan Doherty 2015-16, duo had a best finish of fourth and fifth in 13 tournaments in that time frame
  • Made his international debut in 2009 on the FIVB World Tour
  • Played on the NORCECA Continental Tour in 2013 and 2016, in those two seasons had one silver and bronze medal finishes
  • From 2010-16 on the AVP Tour, has three titles, five second-place finishes and eight third-place finishes
  • Has played on the AVP Tour since 2003, his first tournament title was in 2009, and also earned four third-place finishes that year
  • Made his professional debut in 2003 on the AVP Tour, while still in college
  • Transferred to Pepperdine and was a setter, libero and OH for the National Championship team in 2005
  • Played at Los Angeles Pierce Community College, leading them to a state championship in 2002 and was the tournament MVP

Quick Facts
Began boxing at 17 after passing a muai thai kickboxing gym in the valley in Los Angeles for weeks. She quickly became obsessed with it. She's the first person in her family of hippies to box. She moved to Marquette, Mich., to join the USOEC program in Marquette, Mich., and met her coach Al Mitchell there. She splits time between Marquette and Colorado Springs.

Quick Facts

  • Sport: Para track and field, soccer

  • Specialty: 100-meter, 200-meter, 400-meter and 7-a-side soccer

Biographical Information

Nick Mayhugh is a distinguished para athlete known for his achievements in both soccer and track and field. A diagnosis of cerebral palsy at age 14 set him on a path to becoming one of the most versatile and accomplished para-athletes in the world.

Mayhugh competed on the U.S. men’s Paralympic soccer team, then transitioned to track and field and excelled at the Tokyo 2020 Paralympic Games. 

Passionate about giving back to his community and supporting youth in athletics, Mayhugh delivered a commencement speech at his alma mater in addition to visiting local schools, hospitals and athletic teams.

Olympic Highlights

Mayhugh made a stunning debut at the Tokyo 2020 Paralympic Games. Competing in the T37 classification, he captured three gold medals in the 100-meter final, 200-meter final and the 4x100-meter universal relay final, setting world records in both the 100-meter (10.95 seconds) and 200-meter (21.91 seconds). 

He competed in the Paris 2024 Paralympic Games in both long T38 long jump and T138 100-meter, placing 5th and 7th respectively. 

World Championship Highlights

Mayhugh achieved success at the 2023 World Para Athletics Championships when he secured a bronze medal in the T38 100-meter event.

Parapan American Games Highlights

In 2019, as a member of the U.S. men's Paralympic soccer team, Mayhugh played a key role in leading the team to a historic bronze medal at the Parapan American Games, marking the first time the U.S. had ever medaled in this event. His outstanding performance was further recognized when he was honored as U.S. Soccer Player of the Year with a Disability.

Early Career

Diagnosed with cerebral palsy as a teenager, Mayhugh refused to let the condition limit his ambitions. Supported by his family and coaches, Mayhugh’s early years were filled with hard work and resilience, laying the foundation for his future success in both soccer and track and field.

Paralympic Experience
  • 2-time Paralympian; 4-time Paralympic medalist (3 gold, 1 silver)
    • Paralympic Games Paris 2024, 5th (Long Jump T38 - Men), 7th (100m T38 - Men)
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, gold (100m T37 - Men, 200m T37 - Men, 4 x 100m Medley - Mixed), silver (400m T37 - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2024 – 4th (400m T38 - Men), 7th (100m T38 - Men)
  • Years of participation: 100m T38 - Men 2023, 2024; 400m T38 - Men 2023, 2024; Team - Men 2017, 2019
  • Medals: 1 (1 bronze)
    • Bronze – 2023 (100m T38 - Men)

FUN FACTS

  • Chris’ favorite show on television is Vice News
  • Chris is an outdoor enthusiast who enjoys rock climbing, mountain biking, hiking, skiing and exploring the world
  • His website is: www.chrismazdzer.com

Quick Facts
  • Sophie is the daughter of Kristin and Preston McAfee
  • She has an older sister, Ellie
  • Sophie started diving at age 7 and trains 2 1/2-5 hours a day six days a week
  • She qualified for her first junior nationals at age 8 and competed in her first senior nationals at age 12
  • Her favorite dive is 305C on both 3-meter and 10-meter
  • She likes to dance, bake and travel
  • She has 63 Spotify playlists that can always match her moods, and she has a food Instagram to showcase her love for baking
  • She listens to Imagine Dragons between most dives as their songs have a lot of motivational themes
  • Her favorite food is chocolate chip pancakes
  • Her favorite book is Born a Crime by Trevor Noah
Career Highlights
  • World Junior Championships, Kyiv, 2018 (10m Platform Synchro - Women, 3m Springboard - Women, 10m Platform - Women, 1m Springboard - Women)

bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Position: Defenseman
  • Married his wife Kiley in 2023
  • Father to his son named Rhys
Biographical Information

McAvoy is a defenseman for the Boston Bruins, drafted 14th overall in 2016. Developed through the USA Hockey National Team Development Program and Boston University, he quickly emerged as one of the NHL’s top two-way defensemen. Internationally, he’s won gold at the World Juniors and bronze at the World Championships. McAvoy married Kiley Sullivan in 2023 and became a father in 2025.

  • Years of participation: Team - Men 2017, 2018
  • Medals: 1 (1 bronze)
    • Bronze – 2018 (Team - Men)
Olympic Experience
  • 1-time Olympian; 1-time Olympic medalist (1 gold)
    • Olympic Games Milan 2026, gold (Team - Men)
